title: FIGS Q4 2024 Earnings: Revenue Beats but EPS Misses Expectations
text: FIGS reported Q4 2024 net revenues of $151.8 million, exceeding expectations of $139.49 million. This represented 5% YoY growth and third consecutive quarter of positive scrubwear performance. However, EPS came in at $0.01, missing forecast of $0.03. Full-year revenue reached record $555.6 million, up 2% from 2023, though adjusted EBITDA margins contracted significantly to 9.3% from 15.8% YoY. International business surged 45% for quarter, reaching 16% of net revenues. Repeat customer frequency remained positive for third consecutive quarter. Non-scrubware category rebounded with 13% growth. Strong cash position of $245.1 million with no debt. Customer acquisition headwinds persisted. Gross margins contracted 150 basis points from product mix shifts. Active customer growth slowed to 3% YoY. Operating expenses deleveraged to 67.2% of revenues. Management anticipated low single-digit revenue decline in 2025 from deliberate pullback on promotions to strengthen brand positioning. Expected adjusted EBITDA margins 9-9.5%. Key investments planned include international expansion into Japan and South Korea, Teams B2B platform scaling, and retail development through community hubs.
